Night Vision Devices Night vision devices (also called NVDS) are used by both civilians and soldiers_ They operate number of different principles but the net effect virtual transformation of night into day: Characters using NVDs are not subject to any darkness penalties though other conditionae modifiers still count; such as fOg Or rain_ NVDs nave sevecra drawbacks. Colors cannot be distin- Buished while using a NVD at least not without sophisti cated computer not usually available to grunt in the field. Depth perception also hampered, characteristic that can surprise someone not used to using an NVD On the other hand, the fact that virtually all penalties for darkness are negated the use of one makes Up for those deficiencies Characters fir- ing ranged weapon rCceive +1 cumulative TN Modifier a each range bracket (+1 at short range +2 at medium range and so on); while they receive standard +1 TN Modifier when per- forming any other delicate action that relies on fine eyesight Night vision scopes for weapons are described on 139 Goggles: Almost every modern military issues NVD goggles their soldiers when night combat is expected_ The goggles can be attached helmet or Won headband;
